Cotton_Bacterial_Blight.symptoms: Water-soaked, angular spots on leaves that turn brown or black. Black cankers on stems (blackarm phase) and rotting of bolls.

organic_solution.application: Spray Neem Oil (3-5% concentration) combined with liquid soap emulsifier every 10 days. Also, apply seed treatment with Pseudomonas fluorescens (10g/kg of seed) and spray the crop with the same at 0.2% concentration.
organic_solution.cultural_control: Destroy infected crop residues post-harvest, use certified disease-free seeds, and practice crop rotation with non-host crops.

chemical_solution.application: Spray Copper Oxychloride 50% WP (3.0 g/litre of water) mixed with Streptocycline (100 mg/litre of water) at the initiation of the disease. Repeat after 15 days if necessary.
chemical_solution.precautions: Avoid spraying during hot midday hours to prevent phytotoxicity. Wear protective gear.

Tomato_Early_Blight.symptoms: Dark spots with concentric rings (target-like pattern) on older leaves. Leaves turn yellow and drop off prematurely.

organic_solution.application: Apply Trichoderma viride to the soil and spray foliage with copper-based soap/fungicide approved for organic farming.
organic_solution.cultural_control: Prune lower leaves to prevent splash-up from the soil, apply mulch around the base of the plant, and avoid overhead sprinkler irrigation.

chemical_solution.application: Spray Chlorothalonil 75% WP (2.0 g/litre) or Mancozeb 75% WP (2.5 g/litre) at 7-10 day intervals starting when weather conditions favor disease development.
chemical_solution.precautions: Adhere to the Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) of 5 to 7 days before picking tomatoes.

Tomato_Late_Blight.symptoms: Large, blue-gray water-soaked spots on leaves that rapidly turn brown and dry. White mold appears on the underside of leaves in humid weather.

organic_solution.application: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture at regular intervals during high humidity. Use compost tea to boost leaf surface microbes.
organic_solution.cultural_control: Provide wide spacing for air circulation, grow resistant varieties, and destroy infected plants immediately to prevent airborne spread.

chemical_solution.application: Spray Metalaxyl-M + Mancozeb (2.5 g/litre of water) or Azoxystrobin (1.0 ml/litre of water) immediately upon the first appearance of symptoms.
chemical_solution.precautions: Highly prone to resistance development; alternate with other fungicide classes and do not exceed recommended annual applications.

Corn_Common_Rust.symptoms: Golden-brown to reddish-brown powdery pustules on both upper and lower leaf surfaces, which turn black as the plant matures.

organic_solution.application: Apply wettable sulfur powder (3g/litre) or spray garlic/neem-based botanical formulations early in the morning.
organic_solution.cultural_control: Plant highly resistant hybrid varieties, clear all volunteer plants, and till under crop residues in the off-season.

chemical_solution.application: Spray Propiconazole 25% EC (1.0 ml/litre of water) or Mancozeb (2g/litre of water) at the first sign of rust pustules.
chemical_solution.precautions: Ensure complete canopy coverage, particularly on upper leaf surfaces where rust is most prominent.

Potato_Early_Blight.symptoms: Small brown/black spots on older leaves, forming concentric rings. Tubers may develop dark, sunken lesions.

organic_solution.application: Apply bio-fungicides containing Bacillus subtilis to soil and foliage. Use organic-approved copper hydroxide sprays during rainy spells.
organic_solution.cultural_control: Ensure adequate nitrogen and potassium soil fertilization to prevent plant stress. Practice 3-year crop rotation.

chemical_solution.application: Apply Mancozeb (2.5 g/litre) or Difenoconazole 25% EC (0.5 ml/litre) at 10-14 day intervals.
chemical_solution.precautions: Ensure tubers are fully mature before harvesting to prevent skin damage and secondary infection.

Potato_Late_Blight.symptoms: Water-soaked dark lesions on leaves, surrounded by a pale green halo. Rapid decay of stems and rotting of tubers in soil.

organic_solution.application: Frequent, preventive sprays of copper octanoate. Harvest tubers only in dry weather and store in cool, dry conditions.
organic_solution.cultural_control: Plant certified seed tubers. Hill up soil around potato plants to protect growing tubers from washing spores.

chemical_solution.application: Spray Cymoxanil + Mancozeb (2.0 g/litre) or Dimethomorph (1.0 g/litre) immediately when conditions turn cool and wet.
chemical_solution.precautions: Late blight can destroy crops within days. Act quickly at the earliest weather warnings.

Rice_Blast.symptoms: Spindle-shaped, diamond-like lesions on leaves with grey centers and brown borders. Neck rot causes panicles to fall over.

organic_solution.application: Soak seeds in Pseudomonas fluorescens solution. Spray the crop at tillering and panicle initiation stages with the same at 0.5% concentration.
organic_solution.cultural_control: Avoid excess nitrogen fertilizer, maintain proper water levels in fields, and burn or compost stubbles after harvest.

chemical_solution.application: Apply Tricyclazole (0.6 g/litre of water) or Isoprothiolane 40% EC (1.5 ml/litre of water) at tillering and booting stages.
chemical_solution.precautions: Ensure spray reaches the leaf sheath and base of panicles for neck blast prevention.

Healthy.symptoms: No visible spots, discoloration, or deformities. Leaves are green, firm, and normal in size.

organic_solution.application: Keep up regular watering and apply well-composted organic manure or liquid vermiwash to maintain soil fertility and plant immunity.
organic_solution.cultural_control: Perform regular scouting twice a week to detect early pests/pathogens. Clean tools before moving between fields.

chemical_solution.application: Avoid applying chemical fungicides on healthy crops to preserve beneficial surface microflora and save input costs.
chemical_solution.precautions: Monitor environmental conditions (like continuous rainfall) to anticipate potential disease outbreaks.
